meaning: n.绉戞灄鏂帇[甯岃厞鍚涗富]
examples: As with Sisyphus, the boulder was painstakingly rolled upwards only to tumble back down again in an absurd exercise of futility.
Like Sisyphus, I can never roll the stone up the hill of my expectations.
It's like Sisyphus rolling the ball up the hill, a thirst that can't be quenched.
The researchers' comparison of the struggle for privacy to the eternal plight of Sisyphus?
